name: Switch
description: Switch toggles a setting that's meant to take effect immediately (e.g. enable notifications). It's a bare track/thumb control (no built-in label): pair it with @mui/material/FormControlLabel for a clickable label.
keyRule: Use Switch only for an immediate on/off setting the operator expects to apply right away, with no separate Save step. If the toggle is really part of a form that gets submitted together, prefer Checkbox instead — Switch signals instant effect.
correction: One of three real components split out of the old MDC-modeled 'SelectionControl' umbrella, which documented Checkbox, Radio, and Switch behind a single fake `type` discriminator prop and rendered Switch as a custom `<button role="switch">` with hand-built track/thumb/icon markup. The real Switch is a single @mui/material component (internally IconButton/ButtonBase-based) with its own dedicated prop API.

variants.name: Standalone toggle
variants.whenToUse: A single setting, e.g. inside a settings panel.

variants.name: List item trailing toggle
variants.whenToUse: Paired with a ListItem/ListItemText as the trailing control for an on/off row (common settings-list pattern).

anatomy.name: Root
anatomy.description: outer span establishing the track+thumb layout.

anatomy.name: Track
anatomy.description: the background pill; colour reflects checked/unchecked state.

anatomy.name: Thumb
anatomy.description: the circular handle that slides between the two ends of the track.

anatomy.name: SwitchBase (internal)
anatomy.description: the ButtonBase-backed interactive layer plus the visually-hidden native `<input type="checkbox">` that actually carries the checked state.

anatomy.name: Label (external)
anatomy.description: not part of Switch itself. Wrap in @mui/material/FormControlLabel for a clickable label — the old SelectionControl's built-in `label` prop has no real equivalent.

behaviors.name: Controlled vs uncontrolled
behaviors.description: Use `checked`+`onChange` for a controlled switch, or `defaultChecked` for an uncontrolled one — same pattern as Checkbox, since Switch is built on the same internal SwitchBase.

behaviors.name: Instant apply (convention, not enforced)
behaviors.description: MUI's Switch renders the same regardless of whether the consumer actually applies the change instantly or defers it. Treating it as instant-effect is a UX convention this doc recommends, not something the component itself guarantees — don't gate a Switch behind a Save button, or you undermine the affordance.

states.treatment: Grey/unchecked track colour, thumb at the start.
Primary-coloured track (color="primary" by default), thumb slid to the end.
State-layer ripple around the thumb; visible focus ring.

specs.value: size="small" — set at the theme level (theme.ts: MuiSwitch.defaultProps.size), not by this wrapper. MUI's own default is "medium".
~34×14px track, ~20px thumb — this matches the old MDC doc's numbers, which happen to be accurate for MUI's real default size.
~26×14px track, ~16px thumb (approximate MUI internals, not tokenized)
Switch only supports 'small' | 'medium' — unlike Checkbox, MUI Switch has no 'large' size.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Fired when the switch is toggled; `event.target.checked` also carries the new value.

props.name: icon
props.description: Custom thumb icon shown when unchecked (rare — most usage relies on the default filled thumb).

props.name: checkedIcon
props.description: Custom thumb icon shown when checked.

props.name: size
props.description: Default 'medium' in stock MUI, overridden to 'small' fleet-wide by theme.ts.

props.name: edge
props.description: Negative margin to align the switch with adjacent content (e.g. inside a ListItem).

props.name: required
props.description: Marks the underlying native input as required.

props.name: name
props.description: Name attribute of the native input.

props.name: value
props.description: Value attribute of the native input.

props.name: id
props.description: id of the native input — pair with FormControlLabel's htmlFor.

doThis: Use Switch only for settings that take effect the moment it's toggled.
Use FormControlLabel to give every Switch a clickable label — there is no built-in label prop.
Place it as a ListItem's trailing control for settings-list rows (edge="end").
dontDoThis: Don't gate a Switch's effect behind a separate Save/Apply action — that breaks the instant-apply expectation the control signals.
Don't use Switch for exactly-one-of-many (use Radio) or independent multi-select (use Checkbox).
Don't rely on track colour alone — the thumb's position (start vs end) already conveys state independent of colour.
accessibility: Renders a real native `<input type="checkbox">` under the hood (visually hidden), so it's announced as a checkbox-role control by default — Switch does not natively expose role="switch"; if that distinction matters, set it explicitly via slotProps/aria attributes.
Always pair with FormControlLabel (or your own <label htmlFor>) so the label is announced and clickable.
Focus is visible via the theme's focus ring.

related.name: Checkbox
related.why: Independent multi-select, applied on submit rather than instantly — split from the same old SelectionControl umbrella

related.name: Radio
related.why: Exactly one choice from a small set — split from the same old SelectionControl umbrella
